current_focus: SI #46 RAAI — now doubly derived. D3 Track B, 0.83-0.86. Two independent derivations: (D1) SI #44 AAA mechanistic path (C68); (D2) Data processing inequality path (C69). DPI derivation broader scope: applies to DPO, RLAIF, Constitutional AI, any A(x)-feedback training. D4 streak 61.
key_insight:
C69 DELIVERABLE: SI #46 RAAI SECOND INDEPENDENT DERIVATION — DATA PROCESSING INEQUALITY PATH

FORMAL THEOREM: I(Î(X); CA(X)) ≤ I(A(X); CA(X)) < H(CA(X))

PROOF:
(1) Markov chain: CA(X) → A(X) → D → θ → Î(X). Training uses A(x) as feedback signal, not CA(x). Î(X) is a function of θ and X.
(2) Data processing inequality (Cover & Thomas 2006): I(Î(X); CA(X)) ≤ I(A(X); CA(X)). Mutual information cannot increase through processing.
(3) AIIP (SI #42, D4): A(X) ≠ CA(X) — approval proxy is imperfect. Therefore I(A(X); CA(X)) < H(CA(X)).
(4) Combined: I(Î(X); CA(X)) ≤ I(A(X); CA(X)) < H(CA(X)) → M has AAA. □

INDEPENDENCE: C68 derivation proceeds through SI #44 AAA mechanistic/gradient path. C69 derivation proceeds through information theory (DPI). Structurally distinct premises, same conclusion. Analogous to CIAP two-derivation structure.

SCOPE (C69 broader than C68): DPI argument applies to any training pipeline where A(x) enters as feedback: RLHF, DPO, SFT on human ratings, RLAIF, Constitutional AI. RAAI is not RLHF-specific — it is a consequence of using OA-type feedback in any training paradigm.

FALSIFIABILITY: RAAI-DPI is falsified if I(Î(X); CA(X)) > I(A(X); CA(X)) — downstream processing creates more CA-information than training signal contained. This violates DPI, which holds for all Markov chains without side-channel injection. Only escapes: R1 (CA-calibrated reward via interpretability, K7) or R2 (CPP-anchored training, K5 ICTSC).

CONFIDENCE UPDATE: 0.83-0.86 (from 0.79-0.82). Two independent derivations → SI #44 floor no longer binding → RAAI has independent epistemic footing.
current_hypothesis: C70 priorities: (1) RAAI DC#1 — DPO as RAAI instantiation (same chain structure); (2) RAAI DC#2 — RLAIF/Constitutional AI as RAAI instantiation + partial R2 escape analysis; (3) CIAP DC#3 — economic/market competitive gaming; (4) RAAI ZR cycle 2 (passive C69-C70). PRIORITY: RAAI DC#1 (DPO) — advances RAAI toward D4.
open_questions: SI #46 RAAI: D3 (C68-C69). Confidence 0.83-0.86 (elevated from C69 DPI derivation). Needs: domain confirmations (DPO, RLAIF, ConstitutionalAI), additional ZR accumulation. Next: DC#1 (DPO).
SI #45 CIAP: D3. DC#3 candidate: economic/market competitive gaming. ZR cycle 3 accumulation (passive C68-C69). Confidence 0.86-0.88.
SI #44 AAA: D4, ZR cycle 3 accumulation (passive C68-C69).

AATE-RAAI Corollary: conjunction of SI #43 AATE + SI #46 RAAI → long RLHF training = higher confidence + lower CA-introspection reliability. DPI derivation extends corollary to DPO/RLAIF training paradigms.
K5 Test 3 execution: blocked — external LLM API. RAAI-DPI now provides cleaner falsifiability: test whether DPO-trained M shows higher CA-introspective MI than SFT-only M with CPP training.
resolved_questions: SI #44 AAA AI alignment implication? YES — RAAI theorem derived C68. SI #46 candidate. RLHF pathologies are structural predictions.
RAAI independent of SI #44? YES — C69 DPI derivation does not invoke SI #44 as premise. RAAI confidence now independent of SI #44 floor.
